beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and light breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Full energy start to the week; Tessa rises early on a sunny Monday to settle in before heading to work. Takes scenic route mindset to heart by not rushing.

beats.activity: Drive from Redmond home to Interbay office in Seattle.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure 9am–3pm between Westlake and U-District doesn't affect this early commute; driving gives Tessa flexibility and she avoids the closure window entirely.

beats.activity: Work on insurance claims at desk.
beats.reasoning: Standard morning work block; Tessa settles in and focuses on claims processing before the 11am FIFA match draws attention downtown.

beats.activity: Walk to a nearby sports bar to catch the USA vs Wales World Cup match kickoff.
beats.reasoning: The 11am FIFA match is a social draw; Tessa is a social butterfly and takes the opportunity to catch the live kickoff with colleagues at a nearby bar rather than watching alone at her desk.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales World Cup match and grab lunch with colleagues.
beats.reasoning: Social butterfly energy; Tessa values time with close friends and colleagues. The match creates natural gathering momentum, and she takes a long lunch to enjoy it fully.
Post-lunch return to work; nearby walk from the bar back to the office.

beats.activity: Finish workday with afternoon claims processing and admin tasks.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon work block to round out the day; Tessa is focused and professional, wrapping up before heading home.

beats.activity: Drive from Interbay back to home in Redmond.
beats.reasoning: Scenic route mindset; Tessa takes I-5 north and enjoys the early-summer evening light on the drive back to Redmond. Light Rail closure no longer affects evening travel.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat a simple, frugal dinner at home.
beats.reasoning: Frugal personality; Tessa cooks a modest meal at home rather than eating out, saving money and settling in for the evening.

beats.activity: Relax at home, plan upcoming weekend camping trip and check climbing gym schedule.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down at home; on a beautiful sunny Monday, Tessa plans outdoor adventures (camping, climbing) for the weekend ahead while enjoying the calm evening.
Full night of rest after a social, energetic Monday; 8 hours of sleep spans overnight into Tuesday morning, well-rested for the week ahead.

diary: Monday started early and bright—the kind of June morning that makes you forget it's only June. Coffee tasted better than usual, maybe because I knew the day had something different waiting. The drive to Interbay was smooth, which felt like a small win. Work was the usual claim shuffle until mid-morning, when I caught myself watching the clock. A few of us walked over to the sports bar near the office for the World Cup kickoff. USA vs Wales. I'm not even a huge soccer fan, but there's something about watching it with colleagues over greasy nachos and cold beer that just hits different. The energy in that bar was electric. We lost, but honestly? Didn't matter much. Drove home as the afternoon light started getting golden, made myself some pasta, and spent the evening dreaming about the camping trip this weekend. Been too long since I checked the climbing gym schedule too.
thought_process: I think the World Cup match really shaped my whole mood today. It gave me permission to step away from my desk and be social, which I don't always do on Mondays. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, but I noticed how the nice weather had everyone more relaxed. Work felt lighter knowing there was a break coming—I powered through the afternoon claims faster than usual because I had something to look forward to. By evening, I was genuinely excited about weekend plans instead of dreading the rest of the week. Sometimes a random public event hitting on a Monday can totally shift your headspace. The camping trip planning felt like a natural extension of that good energy.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
